pages.full_text:
We had supper at the local pub, which, together with the church, is just up the lane. The land lady was fierce, the landlord more friendly.
1 lasagna (Lawrence didn't eat any because of ~~and~~ British beef)
4 chicken Kiev, 2 scampi.
2 hot choc fudge ~~pie~~ cake with ice cream
one hot choc f. ~~pie~~cake with cream
one cold choc fudge cake with cream
1 strawberry short cake, one blackberry + apple pie.
Friendly local people. One rescued Charles who was shut behind heavy loo door.
      It rained in the night, but we were able to eat breakfast outside. The loos were blocked.
      We walked along to church at 10.45. V. friendly.
      Back to the van, + immediately set off the 5 miles to Chatsworth.

[margin left:] View of hills above campsite. Climbers + walkers.
[margin right:] Barn next to Calver campsite. Old chapel?
pages.notes: The page number '11' appears at the bottom-right corner, which may be the handwritten page number for this page but positioned unusually low; it could also be a continuation marker. The left margin reads 'View of hills above campsite. Climbers + walkers.' The right margin (near gutter) reads 'Barn next to Calver campsite. Old chapel?' — both appear to be captions for sketches on adjacent pages. 'British beef' has a strikethrough word before it (likely 'and' or similar). Two hot chocolate dessert lines have strikethrough corrections. No date is explicit on this page; given the neighbouring transcript A_0172_R is dated 9 April 1996, and the handwritten page sequence (right page is '6'), this entry is likely April 1996. The campsite is at Calver, near Chatsworth, Derbyshire.

pages.full_text:
[Hand-drawn sketch of a building — a barn or old chapel. The drawing shows a two-storey structure with a large window on the upper left, a round arched doorway in the centre with steps leading up to it, battlemented parapet details at the base, and what appears to be a fireplace or alcove on the upper right. The building is drawn in red/brown ink with careful architectural detail.]

[margin right:] Charles' [?version] overleaf
pages.notes: The page contains a large hand-drawn architectural sketch of a barn or old chapel (consistent with the left-page caption 'Barn next to Calver campsite. Old chapel?'). The handwritten page number '6' appears in the top-right corner. The right margin reads 'Charles' [?version] overleaf' — 'version' is uncertain, could read 'version' or '[?]ersion'; this likely refers to a child's drawing of the same building on the next page. This is a sketch/illustration page, not a route sketch/map. Place context is Calver campsite, Derbyshire.
